skill.name: poster-layout-planner
skill.description: Designs academic poster layouts with structured section placement, visual hierarchy recommendations, and content flow optimization.
skill.summary: AI-assisted academic poster layout planner for research conferences and presentations.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 46 static analyzer findings evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 'backtick execution' detections are markdown code formatting syntax in SKILL.md. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections match no actual crypto code. The 'path traversal' detections occur inside a security checklist that warns against path traversal. The 'hardcoded URL' is a documentation reference link. The 'system reconnaissance' detections match common words. The sole Python script (scripts/main.py, 32 lines) contains no subprocess, network, filesystem, or eval calls — it only constructs a dictionary and prints JSON. The skill's only actual risk factor is documenting how to run Python scripts (external_commands), which is inherent to its purpose as a code-execution skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick Formatting Misidentified as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged standard markdown backtick syntax used for inline code and code block formatting in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are documentation formatting characters, not executable code. All 25 detections occur within markdown code examples showing how to run the Python script.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security Checklist Items Misidentified as Path Traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: Lines 121 and 124 of SKILL.md contain '../' inside a security checklist that actively warns against path traversal attacks. These are defensive documentation items, not vulnerable code patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation URL Misidentified as Hardcoded Credential or Network Call
dangerous_patterns.description: Line 7 of SKILL.md contains a GitHub source repository URL in a markdown blockquote. This is a documentation reference link, not an API endpoint or credential. The skill code makes no network requests.

dangerous_patterns.title: Non-Cryptographic Content Misidentified as Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithms across multiple files, but none of the flagged locations contain any cryptographic code. Files contain only markdown headings, Python docstrings, parameter tables, and bullet-point reference lists. No hashing, encryption, or encoding operations exist in the codebase.

dangerous_patterns.title: Common Words Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged occurrences of common words such as 'path', 'checklist', and 'test' as system reconnaissance indicators. These are ordinary documentation and test-case descriptions with no reconnaissance intent or capability.

content.user_title: Design Academic Poster Layouts with AI
content.value_statement: Academic posters require careful planning of visual hierarchy and section placement. This skill provides structured layout recommendations so your research poster is clear, professional, and well-organized.
actual_capabilities: Generates structured poster layout plans with section placement maps
Recommends visual hierarchy for academic and scientific posters
Suggests poster dimensions and content flow optimization strategies
Produces reproducible JSON-formatted layout plans with design recommendations
Provides readability and contrast guidance for conference poster presentations
limitations: Does not create visual mockups, rendered images, or graphic poster files
Requires manual definition of poster sections and dimensions as input
Cannot verify layout compliance against specific conference submission guidelines
Limited to column-based layout patterns without custom shape arrangements

use_cases.title: First-Time Conference Presenter
use_cases.description: A graduate student preparing their first academic conference poster needs structured guidance on section placement and visual flow. The skill provides a clear layout plan they can hand off to a designer or use in presentation software.

use_cases.title: Departmental Symposium Quick Layout
use_cases.description: A researcher needs a rapid poster layout for an upcoming departmental symposium. The skill generates a reproducible layout plan in seconds, saving time on design decisions and ensuring a professional structure.

use_cases.title: Lab Poster Template Standardization
use_cases.description: A lab manager wants to standardize poster templates across their research group. The skill provides consistent, documented layout plans that the team can reuse for multiple conferences and presentations.
use_cases.target_user: Lab managers and research group leaders

prompt_templates.title: Basic Poster Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a poster layout for a 36x48 inch poster with these sections: Introduction, Methods, Results, Conclusion.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user needing a simple academic poster layout

prompt_templates.title: Clinical Research Poster
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a poster layout plan for a clinical research poster with these sections: Background, Study Design, Patient Demographics, Primary Outcomes, Secondary Outcomes, Limitations, References. The poster is 48x36 inches.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher preparing a clinical study poster for a medical conference

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Subsection Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a poster layout for a 36x48 inch poster. Sections: Title, Authors, Abstract, Introduction, Hypothesis, Methods (split into Data Collection and Analysis), Results (primary and secondary), Discussion, Conclusions, References, Acknowledgments. Optimize for visual flow from top-left to bottom-right.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex research project with detailed methodology sections

prompt_templates.title: Large-Format Multi-Panel Poster
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a poster layout for a 48x72 inch poster with complex multi-panel data presentation. Sections include: Title, Authors, Background, Objectives, Methods (three sub-sections), Results (four figure panels with legends), Statistical Summary Table, Discussion, Study Limitations, Clinical Implications, Future Directions, References, Funding. Ensure logical content flow and optimal use of large format space.
prompt_templates.scenario: Large-format poster with extensive data visualization needs

output_examples.input: Design a poster layout for 36x48 inches with sections: Introduction, Methods, Results, Conclusion
output_examples.output: Your 36x48 inches poster uses a two-column layout. Place the Title and Authors at the top spanning full width. The left column contains Introduction and Methods. The right column contains Results and Conclusion. Keep the title visible from 6 feet away and use high-contrast colors for readability across the presentation room.

output_examples.input: Create a poster layout for a clinical study with sections: Background, Study Design, Results, Discussion, References
output_examples.output: Your clinical study poster uses a two-column format. Top banner: Title and Authors at full width. Left column: Background and Study Design with clear methodological flow. Right column: Results and Discussion with logical progression. Bottom row: References and Acknowledgments. Use consistent font sizes across sections and ensure figures are readable from 4 feet distance.

output_examples.input: Plan a 48x72 inch poster layout with multiple data panels and subsections
output_examples.output: Your large-format 48x72 inches poster uses a three-column layout to maximize space. Top banner: Title and Authors spanning full width. Left column: Introduction, Objectives, and Methods with clear subsection breaks. Center column: Key Results with dedicated figure panels and descriptive legends. Right column: Discussion and Conclusions with take-home messages. Bottom row: References, Acknowledgments, and Funding. For this large format, use 36pt minimum font size for body text and ensure visual elements guide the reader through the narrative flow.

best_practices: Define all poster sections and exact dimensions before requesting a layout plan
Review the generated layout against your specific conference or institution submission guidelines
Use the structured JSON output to maintain consistency across multiple poster revisions
anti_patterns: Do not expect the skill to render visual poster images, graphics, or design files
Do not provide vague section names without specifying precise poster dimensions
Do not skip reviewing layout recommendations against your specific conference requirements

faq.question: What poster sizes does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill accepts any poster dimensions you specify. Common academic sizes include 36x48 inches and 48x72 inches. Provide your dimensions and the skill will adapt the layout plan accordingly.

faq.question: Can this skill create visual poster files or rendered images?
faq.answer: No. The skill produces a layout plan describing section placement and design recommendations in structured format. You must use separate design software such as PowerPoint, Adobe Illustrator, or Canva to create the visual poster.

faq.question: Does this skill follow specific conference or journal guidelines?
faq.answer: The skill provides general academic poster layout recommendations based on standard design principles. You should verify the output against your specific conference, institution, or journal submission guidelines before finalizing your poster.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide to get a layout plan?
faq.answer: You need to provide the poster dimensions and a list of content sections such as Introduction, Methods, Results, and Conclusion. More detailed section lists produce more tailored layout recommendations.

faq.question: Can the skill handle complex multi-panel layouts with figures and tables?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can plan layouts for posters with multiple data panels, figure legends, statistical tables, and subsections. Provide all sections and panels in your input for the most accurate layout plan.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill returns a structured plan with a layout description, a section placement map showing where each section goes, and design recommendations covering readability, contrast, and content flow.
